diagrams.description: Constitutional Law is the body of law that defines the structure, powers, and limits of government institutions, and the relationship between the state and individuals. This flowchart illustrates its systematic operation: the ultimate goal is to uphold constitutional supremacy, protect fundamental rights, and ensure limited government. It integrates core principles (rule of law, separation of powers, federalism, fundamental rights) with key doctrines (judicial review, due process, equal protection, proportionality). Sources include the constitutional text, judicial precedents, conventions, and international treaties. Constitutional interpretation and litigation involve various actors (courts, legislatures, executives) and lead to outcomes such as legal certainty, democratic governance, and the enforcement of rights.
